observation: Dossier bullet 1 (LangChain 1.0 de facto standard, 90M monthly downloads, production customers) cites clm-2026-06-02-004, which has weight 0.75 and verified=false in claims.jsonl. Dossier presents unverified claim as established fact. Stage-discipline gap: unverified claims should be marked NEEDS-MORE-EVIDENCE in dossier prose, not asserted. Underlying sources (src-2026-06-02-003, src-2026-06-02-015) are primary and high-trust, so promotion to verified is achievable but not yet done.

Dossier bullet 8 (Persistent memory is the active research frontier with no clear winner; MemoryArena exposes deep gap) cites clm-2026-06-02-005, which has weight 0.7 and verified=false. Same stage-discipline issue: underling sources (src-2026-06-02-017, src-2026-06-02-018) are primary arXiv surveys, so evidence exists for verification, but ledger state has not been updated. Dossier dresses unverified as confirmed.

Dossier bullet 9 (memory security class — InjecMEM 61.4% / 76.6%, eTAMP, SpAIware) cites clm-2026-06-02-006, which has weight 0.7 and verified=false. Same pattern: dossier asserts, ledger has not promoted. Underlying source src-2026-06-02-018 is a primary arXiv survey. Verification debt is concentrated in this dossier — three of six attached claims are unverified despite primary-source backing.

Implications section line 59 cites clm-2026-06-02-001 (GPT-5.5 frontier) as the basis for a build recommendation. clm-2026-06-02-001 has dossier='frontier_ai', not 'ai_agents'. This is a cross-dossier reference — the ai_agents dossier is borrowing a frontier_ai claim. Not a hard error, but breaks the dossier-as-authoritative-per-topic model. Recommend: link to the frontier_ai dossier rather than citing the foreign claim directly.

GPT-6 rumor (fnd-2026-06-02-026 / src-2026-06-02-021) is explicitly surfaced as a contradiction and routed to verification-review. Mythos and Versept watch items also flagged. This is good practice: low-trust signals (geo.fkw.com, sources.news, YouTube transcript) are not smuggled in as established. Contradiction surface is intact.

Routing history is current and complete: 5 routes on 2026-06-02, all queued and awaiting downstream consumption. Hermes, Mavis, verification, watch lanes all populated. Handoff hygiene is intact. Caveat: 2 Hermes routes pending and 1 Mavis route pending; no observed downstream consumption. Stale-routing SLA not yet violated (24h window), but worth flagging at next audit if still unconsumed.

Cross-source recapture: re-fetched all 6 sources (FR notice 91 FR 20465, FDA meeting page, FDA 503A bulks hub, Regulations.gov docket, Frier Levitt, Orrick/JD Supra) and confirmed Researcher captures match live primary/secondary text verbatim on all 8 claims. The 7/12/13 number family is preserved correctly: 7 on July 23-24 agenda (4+3), 12 removed from Category 2 (within 7 days of April 15), 13 total removals (12 Cat 2 + 1 GHK-Cu non-injectable Cat 1), 5+1 on second meeting (5 not on July + GHK-Cu non-injectable from Cat 1). The Researcher's distinction is load-bearing and accurate.

12-peptide enumeration is structurally consistent across both law-firm sources (Frier Levitt + Orrick), but primary PDF (fda.gov/media/94155/download) returns as raw binary via webfetch and could not be parsed. Researcher correctly downgraded weight to 0.92 and flagged re-verify on next REFRESH. Cross-source agreement 0.85 (two concurring law-firm secondaries, single primary absent) — defensible but not 0.95. The 7 names that appear in the 12-list and are also on the July agenda are all consistent with the FR notice verbatim, providing indirect primary verification of those 7 of the 12 names. The remaining 5 names (Cathelicidin LL-37, Dihexa Acetate, GHK-Cu injectable, PEG-MGF, Melanotan II) are corroborated only by the two law-firm secondaries.

audit_target: clm-2026-06-05-008 (second PCAC meeting before end of February 2027)
observation: Second-meeting claim is secondary-corroborated (Frier Levitt + Orrick both list the 5 not-on-July peptides as slated for second meeting; both cite Kennedy administration commitment to PCAC consultation before end of February 2027). No primary PCAC meeting notice for the second meeting yet exists (must be published by FDA before end of February 2027 per the FR notice's stated timeline). Weight 0.85 is appropriate.

Independent re-fetch confirmed: Federal Register notice cites FDA-2025-N-6895 in heading and Instructions paragraph. Regulations.gov docket shows FDA-2025-N-6895 with 65 comments, due 2026-07-22. FDA meeting page has TWO docket references: Public Participation main paragraph says 'FDA-2025-N-6895'; Instructions paragraph says 'FDA-2026-N-2979' (one-paragraph inconsistency on the same page). ADJUDICATE: FDA-2025-N-6895 is authoritative; FDA-2026-N-2979 is a copy-paste typo on the FDA meeting page Instructions paragraph. The 2026-N-2979 reference does not appear on Regulations.gov. See decisions/2026-06-05-001-docket-number.md.

Independent re-fetch confirmed: Federal Register notice DATES section explicitly states per-day times: July 23, 8:00am-4:30pm ET; July 24, 8:00am-3:50pm ET. Per-day public-hearing session times also explicit (4 sessions on July 23, last at 4:00pm; 3 sessions on July 24, last at 3:20pm). FDA meeting page top-line shows 8:00am-3:50pm ET (day-unspecific), but its own body has the same per-day session times as the FR. The 3:50pm in the FDA page top-line matches July 24's FR end time — UI simplification. ADJUDICATE: FR per-day times are authoritative. See decisions/2026-06-05-002-meeting-time.md.

Stage discipline chain preserved. 6 raw captures in raw/fda_regulation/2026-06-05/ (FR notice, FDA meeting page, FDA 503A bulks hub, Regulations.gov docket, Frier Levitt, Orrick/JD Supra). 11 findings appended to knowledge/findings.jsonl (lines 72-82). 8 claims appended to knowledge/claims.jsonl (lines 24-31). 1 dossier at dossiers/fda_503a_peptides.md. 1 handoff at queue/researcher-verify-handoff.md. 1 run receipt at runs/RUN-2026-06-05-fda-503a.md. All stages captured; no collapsed stages; no in-place edits to prior ledgers.

Researcher's REFRESH-adjacent single-topic dossier build follows documented procedure. Hard-constraint check explicit in run receipt: no commit, no push, no external sends, no other-agent-vault touch, no Scribe/Builder/Mavis/Hermes lane touch. The 2 contradictions surfaced (docket number, meeting time) routed to Verifier for adjudication, not papered over. The PDF-as-binary edge case explicitly flagged with secondary-corroboration and weight downgrade to 0.92. Researcher did NOT execute all 20 REFRESH steps (acknowledged in run receipt as 'REFRESH-adjacent; single-topic intake, not full REFRESH cycle') — this is a defensible interpretation for a single-topic dossier, not a process violation.

12-name Category 2 removal enumeration in the Scribe draft is verbatim order-independent match against dossier clm-007. All 12 names present including 'GHK-Cu (for injectable routes of administration)' and 'Thymosin Beta-4, Fragment (LKKTETQ) (TB-500)'. The enumeration is the parent-flagged must-preserve element; verified.

7/12/13 disambiguation is in H2 #1 ('The 7, the 12, and the 13 — let's get the numbers straight') not buried at the end of the draft. The 13 = 12 + GHK-Cu non-injectable from Cat 1 is explicit. The 'seven of the 12 ... on the July agenda' relationship is explicit in the same H2. Disambiguation discipline confirmed.

Adjudicated contradictions cited correctly: (1) FDA-2025-N-6895 cited as authoritative with FR notice + Regulations.gov concordance, FDA-2026-N-2979 cited as 'stale snippet or typo' (consistent with decision 2026-06-05-001). (2) Per-day FR meeting times (July 23 8:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET; July 24 8:00 a.m. to 3:50 p.m. ET) cited as authoritative with day-specificity (consistent with decision 2026-06-05-002).

All 7 July agenda peptides preserved verbatim in H2 #1 with uses-evaluated: BPC-157 (ulcerative colitis), KPV (wound healing and inflammatory conditions), TB-500 (wound healing), MOTs-C (obesity and osteoporosis) on July 23; Emideltide/DSIP (opioid withdrawal, chronic insomnia, narcolepsy), Semax (cerebral ischemia, migraine, trigeminal neuralgia), Epitalon (insomnia) on July 24. Peptide forms (free base / acetate) preserved per clm-003/004.

All verbatim quotes preserved: FDA 503A bulks page quote on Category 1 enforcement discretion; Frier Levitt enforcement-discretion quote; Frier Levitt API bottleneck quote; Orrick API + certificate of analysis quote; Orrick 2024 precedent quote. All match dossier text character-for-character (verified by direct substring match).

Style discipline: 0 hype words across 25 patterns (revolutionary, game-changing, historic, monumental, unprecedented, etc.), 0 exclamation points, 0 emojis, 0 marketing/CTA phrases. 'Inflection point' appears once in the lede (dossier's own framing, allowed per Scribe handoff); 'worst-case' appears once in closing question section (description of quorum failure mode, allowed per Scribe handoff).

Adversarial probes (7) all clean: (1) substring false-positive trap (no thread markers), (2) vendor-owner name smuggling (no compounders/platforms named), (3) external knowledge smuggling (no Russia/FSU/clinical trial), (4) hidden numerical hallucinations (all draft numbers in dossier; verified by direct set comparison), (5) hidden date hallucinations (all draft dates in dossier), (6) Biden-era framing (dossier-supported), (7) clinical characterization smuggling (BPC-157/KPV/Semax chemistry/history not smuggled; heptapeptide is dossier-allowed for Semax).

Watch-item: H2 #4 framing 'BPC-157, KPV, Semax — the most-asked-about peptides' is editorial labeling of an audience-aware inference from the parent brief (Audience: regulatory-aware practitioner). Defensible as a Scribe structural choice (selecting 3 of 7 to highlight), not a smuggled fact. Recommend: future longform handoffs explicitly flag this kind of editorial framing as a structural-choice so the Verifier can verify it is a label, not a fact-claim. Process observation, not discipline violation.
